content_markdown: Which assumption is part of the molecular kinetic model of ideal gases?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present in the image.

options.label: A
options.text: The work done on a system equals the change in kinetic energy of the system.

options.label: B
options.text: The volume of a gas results from adding the volume of the individual molecules.

options.label: C
options.text: A gas is made up of tiny identical particles in constant random motion.

options.label: D
options.text: All particles in a gas have kinetic and potential energy.

vibe_explanation: The kinetic molecular theory of gases relies on several key postulates: particles are in constant random motion, collisions are perfectly elastic, and the volume of the particles themselves is negligible compared to the container volume. Option C directly states one of these fundamental assumptions. Option B is incorrect because ideal gas particles are assumed to have negligible volume. Option D is incorrect because ideal gases are assumed to have no intermolecular forces, meaning they have zero potential energy.
